comment_text.sn35.52:1.1: The root sense of upassaṭṭha (“contaminated”) is something that has been poured upon, or on which something has been let loose (AN 5.192:8.2). Cf. Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 1.3.6: “They contaminated (upāsṛjan) those gods with evil”, the evil being “unfit” (apratirūpaṁ) sights, sounds, etc.

root_text.sn35.52:1.3: Cakkhu, bhikkhave, upassaṭṭhaṁ, rūpā upassaṭṭhā, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ upassaṭṭhaṁ, cakkhusamphasso upassaṭṭho.
root_text.sn35.52:1.4: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi upassaṭṭhaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.52:1.5: jivhā upassaṭṭhā, rasā upassaṭṭhā, jivhāviññāṇaṁ upassaṭṭhaṁ, jivhāsamphasso upassaṭṭho.
root_text.sn35.52:1.6: Yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi upassaṭṭhaṁ.
root_text.sn35.52:1.8: mano upassaṭṭho, dhammā upassaṭṭhā, manoviññāṇaṁ upassaṭṭhaṁ, manosamphasso upassaṭṭho.
root_text.sn35.52:1.9: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi upassaṭṭhaṁ.
root_text.sn35.52:1.10: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.52:1.11: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.52:1.12: manasmimpi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati, manoviññāṇepi nibbindati, manosamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.52:1.13: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.52:1.14: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.52:1.15: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.52:1.2: And what is the all that is contaminated?
translation_text.sn35.52:1.3: The eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact are contaminated.
translation_text.sn35.52:1.4: And the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises conditioned by eye contact is also contaminated.
translation_text.sn35.52:1.8: The mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and mind contact are contaminated.
translation_text.sn35.52:1.9: And the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises conditioned by mind contact is also contaminated.
translation_text.sn35.52:1.10: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned …
translation_text.sn35.52:1.15: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”

variant_text.sn35.52:2.4: abhiññeyyapariññeyyaṁ → abhiññeyyaṁ pariññeyyaṁ (bj, sya-all, km); abhiññātaṁ pariññeyyaṁ (pts1ed, mr)
